name: Urbino
author: Abstract Games factory (game by Dieter Stein, 2018)
description: Urbino (Dieter Stein, 2018): two players jointly build a town on a 9x9 board using two SHARED architect figures. Each turn, optionally teleport one architect to any empty square, then you MUST erect one of your buildings (house=1, palace=2, tower=3) on a square both architects can see along clear queen-lines. Districts (orthogonally connected buildings) may hold at most one connected block per player, and towers/palaces may never sit orthogonally next to a building of the same type. When both players must skip in a row, each two-coloured district scores its full value for whichever player contributed the higher total. Optional Monuments variant: lines of three (H-H-H, P-H-P, T-P-T) score double.
